text: I feel like I am caught between two worlds: one of sterile formality and one of creative curiosity. The first space—an entrance to a conference or presentation room—makes me conscious of conventions, of the order and rules that structure knowledge and its exchange. There is anticipation in the air, a sense of expectation. Yet, stepping into the second room, I am greeted by vibrant colors and the invitation to reflect, to question, and to imagine. The juxtaposition fills me with both wonder and restlessness; I wonder how innovation might arise from structure, and whether true creativity can flourish within such defined boundaries. I feel alive, tinged by an undercurrent of static energy and possibility.
